Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: Goal: Create a Python dictionary representing a Temporal Profile for a specific supply chain process. Definition: The temporal profile maps pairs of activities tuples to average_time, standard_deviation representing the time elapsed between them in process executions. It includes eventually following pairs, not just direct successors. Context: Global supply chain for high-tech electronics. Activities: SS, OP, RC, QI, CA, PT, PK, WS, DT, AS. Constraint: Do not use explicit numerical temporal information in the prompt meaning I need to estimate reasonable real-world values myself. Output: A Python dictionary with tuple keys and tuple values avg, stdev. Requirement: Include a representative subset, ensuring complexity pairs separated by multiple steps.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Determine Reasonable Time Scales: High-tech electronics supply chain involves international shipping, manufacturing queues, testing, etc. SS -> OP: Very short hours to a day. OP -> RC: Long Days to weeks. RC -> QI: Short hours to a day. QI -> CA: Short hours to days, waiting for assembly line. CA -> PT: Short hours. PT -> PK: Short hours. PK -> WS: Short hours to days. WS -> DT: Variable days to weeks depending on demand/logistics. DT -> AS: Long months to years, after sale. Unit: Seconds is standard for process mining as per the example 86400 = 1 day. I will use seconds.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 3. Select Activity Pairs: I need to cover direct successors and non-direct successors to show the eventually follow aspect. Direct/Close: SS-OP, OP-RC, RC-QI, QI-CA, CA-PT, PT-PK, PK-WS, WS-DT, DT-AS. Intermediate: SS-RC, OP-QI, RC-CA, CA-PK, PK-DT. Long-range: SS-CA, OP-PT, RC-DT, WS-AS, SS-DT.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Estimate Values in Seconds: 1 hour = 3600 1 day = 86400 1 week = 604800 1 month approx = 2592000 SS -> OP: Decision to order. 2 hours avg. Stdev 1 hour. OP -> RC: Procurement lead time. 10 days avg. Stdev 3 days. RC -> QI: Inspection scheduling. 4 hours avg. Stdev 2 hours. QI -> CA: Assembly queue. 1 day avg. Stdev 12 hours. CA -> PT: Testing queue. 2 hours avg. Stdev 1 hour. PT -> PK: Packaging prep. 1 hour avg. Stdev 30 mins. PK -> WS: Storage entry. 2 hours avg. Stdev 1 hour. WS -> DT: Order fulfillment wait. 3 days avg. Stdev 2 days. DT -> AS: Post-sale support window. 6 months avg. Stdev 3 months.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Non-Adjacent Pairs Cumulative logic: SS -> RC: SS->OP + OP->RC. 10 days + 2 hours. Avg: 871200 Stdev: 260000. OP -> CA: OP->RC + RC->QI + QI->CA. 10 days + 4 hrs + 1 day. Avg: 950000. Stdev: 300000. RC -> PT: RC->QI + QI->CA + CA->PT. Avg: 100000. Stdev: 50000. CA -> WS: CA->PT + PT->PK + PK->WS. Avg: 18000. Stdev: 10000. PK -> DT: PK->WS + WS->DT. Avg: 270000. Stdev: 180000. SS -> DT: End of manufacturing to shipment. Avg: 1209600 Stdev: 400000. QI -> PT: QI->CA + CA->PT. Avg: 93600. Stdev: 45000. WS -> AS: Storage to Support. Avg: 15552000. Stdev: 8000000.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5. Review and Refine: Check consistency. If A->B is 1 day and B->C is 1 day, A->C should be roughly 2 days. Ensure the dictionary syntax is correct. Ensure all activity codes match the prompt. Make sure the values are in seconds. Self-Correction on AS: After-Sales Support usually happens after the customer receives the product DT. The time between DT and AS could be very long warranty period. Lets keep it high. Self-Correction on Stdev: Stdev should generally increase as the gap between activities increases more variance accumulates.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. Final Check: Does this meet the TEMPORAL PROFILE definition? Yes, it maps pairs to avg, stdev. Does it cover the scenario? Yes. Does it include non-direct pairs? Yes. Flow: SS -> OP -> RC -> QI -> CA -> PT -> PK -> WS -> DT -> AS. Pairs to include: SS-OP, OP-RC, RC-QI, QI-CA, CA-PT, PT-PK, PK-WS, WS-DT, DT-AS, SS-RC, OP-CA, RC-PT, CA-WS, PK-DT, SS-PT, WS-AS, OP-DT, RC-AS. This covers the requirement for complexity by considering pairs of activities that may be separated by multiple steps. Lets calculate rough sums for consistency check.
